name: Jacob Devlin
key_contributions: Created BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) in 2018
BERT demonstrated that pre-training bidirectional transformers on unlabeled text produced state-of-the-art results across NLP tasks
Introduced masked language modeling as a pre-training objective
BERT became the most widely used NLP model in industry for several years
Showed that the encoder side of the Transformer was immensely powerful for understanding tasks
summary: Jacob Devlin created BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) at Google in 2018, one of the most impactful applications of the Transformer architecture. BERT demonstrated that pre-training a bidirectional Transformer encoder on large amounts of unlabeled text, using masked language modeling, produced representations that could be fine-tuned to achieve state-of-the-art results on virtually every NLP benchmark. BERT spawned an entire family of encoder-only models (RoBERTa, ALBERT, DistilBERT, DeBERTa) and became the backbone of Google Search for years.
